exercises.title: Library System: Book and Member Classes
exercises.description: Build the core Book and Member classes for a library system. Apply encapsulation (properties), __str__, and __eq__ to make them production-quality.
exercises.starterCode:
class Book:
    def __init__(self, title, author, isbn):
        self.title = title
        self.author = author
        self.isbn = isbn
        self._available = True

    @property
    def available(self):
        # TODO: Return self._available
        pass

    def checkout(self):
        # TODO: If not available, raise ValueError(f"'{title}' is already checked out")
        # TODO: Set _available = False
        pass

    def return_book(self):
        # TODO: Set _available = True
        pass

    def __str__(self):
        # TODO: Return "'Clean Code' by Robert Martin [Available]"
        # Status should be 'Available' or 'Checked out'
        pass

    def __eq__(self, other):
        # TODO: Two books are equal if they have the same isbn
        pass


class Member:
    _count = 0

    def __init__(self, name, email):
        Member._count += 1
        self.member_id = f"MEM{Member._count:04d}"
        self.name = name
        self.email = email
        self._borrowed = []

    @property
    def borrowed_count(self):
        # TODO: Return number of borrowed books
        pass

    def borrow(self, book):
        # TODO: Call book.checkout() and append book to self._borrowed
        pass

    def __str__(self):
        # TODO: Return "MEM0001: Alice Smith (1 books borrowed)"
        pass


# Test the classes
book1 = Book("Clean Code", "Robert Martin", "978-0132350884")
book2 = Book("Python Crash Course", "Eric Matthes", "978-1593276034")

alice = Member("Alice Smith", "alice@example.com")
bob = Member("Bob Jones", "bob@example.com")

print(book1)
alice.borrow(book1)
print(book1)
print(alice)

try:
    bob.borrow(book1)  # Should fail - already checked out
except ValueError as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")

book1.return_book()
print(book1)
print(book1 == Book("Other Book", "Author", "978-0132350884"))
exercises.expectedOutput:
'Clean Code' by Robert Martin [Available]
'Clean Code' by Robert Martin [Checked out]
MEM0001: Alice Smith (1 books borrowed)
Error: 'Clean Code' is already checked out
'Clean Code' by Robert Martin [Available]
True
hints: def checkout: if not self._available: raise ValueError(f"'{self.title}' is already checked out")
__str__ of Book: status = 'Available' if self._available else 'Checked out'
__eq__: return isinstance(other, Book) and self.isbn == other.isbn

exercises.title: Polymorphic Payment System
exercises.description: Build a PaymentMethod base class with CreditCard and PayPal subclasses. Each validates and processes differently, but a checkout() function works with any payment type.
exercises.expectedOutput:
Payment successful: Charged $49.99 to card ending in 3456
Payment successful: Paid $29.99 via PayPal (alice@example.com)
Error: Invalid payment
hints: PayPal.validate: return super().validate() and '@' in self.email
CreditCard._execute: return f'Charged ${self.amount:.2f} to card ending in {self.card_last4}'

warmup.title: Quick Recall: OOP All Pillars
warmup.description: This warmup covers all 4 OOP pillars in one example. What does it print?
